options.name: -Description
options.description: Specifies a description of the alias. You can type any string. If the description includes spaces, enclose it in quotation marks.

options.name: -Force
options.description: Indicates that the cmdlet acts like `Set-Alias` if the alias named already exists.

options.name: -Name
options.description: Specifies the new alias. You can use any alphanumeric characters in an alias, but the first character cannot be a number.

options.name: -Option
options.description: Specifies the value of the Options property of the alias. Valid values are:

options.name: -PassThru
options.description: Returns an object representing the item with which you are working. By default, this cmdlet does not generate any output.

options.name: -Scope
options.description: Specifies the scope of the new alias. The acceptable values for this parameter are:

options.name: -Value
options.description: Specifies the name of the cmdlet or command element that is being aliased.

name: -Confirm
-cf
options.description: Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.

name: -WhatIf
-wi
options.description: Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
